DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF COMPLAINT THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION (FALSE REPRESENTATION) AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES ONLY AS TO DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION (TRANSACTION ID # 29234068)

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO NEGLIGENCE AND STRICT LIABILITY CAUSES OF ACTION (ISSUES 1 AND 2) IS DENIED. UNION CARBIDE DID NOT PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE THAT: 1) IT WAS REASONABLE FOR UNION CARBIDE TO RELY ON HAMILTON TO WARN PLAINTIFF, 2) UNION CARBIDE KNEW OF HAMILTON'S LEVEL OF SOPHISTICATION, OR 3) PLAINTIFF WAS A SOPHISTICATED USER OF ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS. (WILLIS V. RAYMARK INDUSTRIES, INC. (4TH CIR. 1990) 905 F.2D 793). CONSEQUENTLY, WHETHER CALLED A SOPHISTICATED USER OR A SOPHISTICATED INTERMEDIARY DEFENSE, THE DEFENSE HAS NOT BEEN SHOWN TO APPLY HERE AS A MATTER OF LAW. UNION CARBIDE'S MOTION AS TO FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES (ISSUES 3 AND 4) IS MOOT AS THOSE CLAIMS HAVE BEEN DISMISSED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: MARY ELLEN SCHERER, CSR # 5208

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Feb-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Jerry Whetman
No other appearances.

RULING:
Defendant Union Carbide Corporation's motion for summary adjudication as to negligence and strict liability causes of action (issues 1 and 2). No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. Union Carbide did not present any evidence that: 1) it was reasonable for Union Carbide to rely on Hamilton to warn plaintiff, 2) Union Carbide knew of Hamilton's level of sophistication, or 3) plaintiff was a sophisticated user of asbestos-containing products. (Willis v. Raymark Industries, Inc. (4th Cir. 1990) 905 F.2d 793). Consequently, whether called a sophisticated user or a sophisticated intermediary defense, the defense has not been shown to apply here as a matter of law. Union Carbide's motion as to false representation and punitive damages (issues 3 and 4) is moot as those claims have been dismissed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Mary Ellen Scherer, CSR # 5208
